# Realtime Driver Tracking

> Real-time GPS location tracking for drivers and vehicles with position history and live map updates

**Category:** Integration · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** fleet · gps · tracking · real-time · location · telematics

## What this does

Real-time GPS location tracking for drivers and vehicles with position history and live map updates

Specifies 5 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **position_uuid** *(text, required)* — Position Record ID
- **subject_uuid** *(text, required)* — Subject (Driver/Vehicle)
- **subject_type** *(text, required)* — Subject Type
- **order_uuid** *(text, optional)* — Active Order
- **destination_uuid** *(text, optional)* — Current Destination
- **coordinates** *(json, required)* — GPS Coordinates
- **heading** *(number, optional)* — Heading (degrees)
- **bearing** *(number, optional)* — Bearing to Destination
- **speed** *(number, optional)* — Speed
- **altitude** *(number, optional)* — Altitude
- **device_id** *(text, optional)* — Device ID
- **device_type** *(select, optional)* — Device Type
- **data_frequency** *(text, optional)* — Update Interval

## What must be true

- **valid_coordinates:** Location updates must include valid GPS coordinates (latitude within -90..90, longitude within -180..180)
- **immutable_positions:** Position records are immutable once stored; they form an audit trail of movement
- **update_frequency:** Driver location is updated at the configured device frequency (default every 30 seconds on active orders)
- **realtime_broadcast:** Location data is broadcast in real time to authorized dispatchers via WebSocket
- **retention_policy:** Position records are retained for the organization's configured retention period
- **stale_flag:** Stale positions older than 5 minutes should be flagged as potentially outdated on maps
- **geometry_storage:** GPS coordinates are stored as geospatial geometry for spatial query performance
- **org_isolation:** Location data is only visible to dispatchers within the same organization
- **driver_consent:** Drivers must consent to location tracking as part of onboarding
- **device_source_recorded:** Vehicle telematics devices can push location updates; position source is recorded

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Position Recorded** — when coordinates exists; subject_uuid exists, then Position recorded and broadcast to live map.
- **Driver Arrives At Waypoint** — when driver coordinates are within arrival radius of current waypoint; order_uuid exists, then Geofence arrival detected and order status updated.
- **Driver Departed From Location** — when driver was at a waypoint location; driver coordinates moved beyond departure radius, then Driver departure from waypoint detected.

**❌ Failure paths**

- **Invalid Coordinates** — when latitude is outside range -90..90 OR longitude is outside range -180..180, then Position rejected — invalid GPS coordinates. *(error: `TRACKING_INVALID_COORDINATES`)*
- **Unauthorized Tracking** — when requestor does not belong to the same organization as the driver, then Location data access denied. *(error: `TRACKING_ACCESS_DENIED`)*

## Errors it can return

- `TRACKING_INVALID_COORDINATES` — Invalid GPS coordinates provided.
- `TRACKING_ACCESS_DENIED` — You do not have permission to view this location.
- `TRACKING_SUBJECT_NOT_FOUND` — Tracked subject not found.

## Connects to

- **driver-profile** *(required)* — Driver profile holds the current location field
- **order-lifecycle** *(required)* — Position updates are linked to active orders
- **route-planning** *(required)* — Waypoint geofences are derived from the route
- **dispatch-driver-assignment** *(recommended)* — Nearest-driver calculation requires real-time positions
- **fleet-performance-dashboard** *(optional)* — Live map shows all driver positions on the dashboard
